title: xeraea conica
intro: Xeraea conica is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is also known as the "desert marigold" or "Mexican gold poppy." The Xeraea conica plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of Xeraea conica can be made into teas, capsules, or tinctures, and are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
